claim: ChatGPT Atlas browser integrates Chat GPT history in sidebar, supports agent mode for full browser control with user approval per task.
actionable: Use Atlas agent mode for complex multi-tab research tasks; monitor each action to build trust in automation.

claim: Atlas memory feature learns from browsing history to suggest relevant tasks and improve search; more data improves personalization.
actionable: Use Atlas regularly to train memory model; clear sensitive history if privacy is concern before AI learns from it.

claim: Atlas uses Google-powered tabs (Web, Images, Videos, News) alongside Chat GPT summary tab; Google powers speed and accuracy for traditional search.
actionable: Switch to Google tabs when you know exact destination; use Chat GPT tab for synthesis and research.

claim: Agent mode shows cursor and step-by-step actions before execution, building trust through transparency of model decisions.
actionable: Review agent trajectory before confirming complex tasks; pause and correct agent if it diverges from intent.

claim: Atlas does not execute purchases without human confirmation; agent prepares order but waits for user to complete payment.
actionable: Configure purchase approvals as final manual step in agent workflows involving financial transactions.

claim: Atlas available on Mac only; Windows release coming soon; agent mode available only in Plus/Pro plans.
actionable: Verify OS and subscription tier before recommending Atlas to team; plan for Windows users.

claim: Atlas enables in-page Chat GPT assistant on any website for summarization, tasks, and agent invocation with full page context.
actionable: Leverage page context in agent prompts; ask agent to summarize, extract, or take action on current page.
